idea-plugin.name: Javelin - An SBFL Tool for IntelliJ
idea-plugin.description:
Javelin: Spectrum-Based Fault Localization for Java

Javelin automates Spectrum-Based Fault Localization (SBFL) for Java projects. It instruments your JUnit tests, builds a coverage spectrum, and ranks every source line or method by suspiciousness, so you can jump straight to the bug instead of searching for it.

The plugin bundles its own analysis engine and runs it using IntelliJ's bundled JBR. No separate JDK 21 installation is required. Projects using Java 8 through Java 21 are supported.

Key Features

  • One-click analysis via the Tools menu or Ctrl+Shift+J
  • 4-tier visual highlighting with red, orange, yellow, and green bands in the editor, gutter, and scrollbar
  • Results table that is sortable, filterable, with double-click navigation to source
  • Export to CSV with rank, score, percentile, and band classification
  • Status-bar readiness widget for instant project health checks before analysis
  • Run configurations to save and reuse custom analysis settings
  • Auto-detect paths for Gradle and Maven project layouts
  • Persistent settings saved across sessions via Settings > Tools > Javelin

Algorithm Support

  • Ochiai (default): standard SBFL algorithm using coverage spectrum
  • Ochiai-MS (experimental): mutation-aware variant integrating PITest mutation analysis for enhanced fault localization

Output Options

  • Granularity: Statement-level (default, ranks individual lines) or method-level (aggregates to methods using max score)
  • Ranking: Dense (default, recommended for debugging) or average/MID (for SBFL evaluation and EXAM scores)
  • Offline mode: Pre-instruments bytecode to avoid Java agent conflicts with Mockito-inline, ByteBuddy, PowerMock, JMockit, or AspectJ
  • Class name notation: Inner and nested classes use JVM naming with $ (e.g., SearchQuery$Builder). Method-level results use # to separate class and method (e.g., Calculator#compute).

Getting Started

  1. Open a Java project with at least one failing JUnit test
  2. Build the project (Build > Build Project or Ctrl+F9)
  3. Press Ctrl+Shift+J or go to Tools > Run Javelin Analysis
  4. Click Auto-Detect in the configuration panel if paths are not set, then click Run Javelin
  5. Suspicious lines are highlighted in the editor and ranked in the Javelin tool window

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2025.1 through 2025.3.x (Community or Ultimate)
  • A compiled Java project (Java 8 or later) with JUnit tests
  • At least one failing test (SBFL requires a fault to localize)

Documentation: GitHub README
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.4.0

  • GREEN band now indicates lines covered by tests but not implicated by failing tests (score = 0), separate from percentile-based Red/Orange/Yellow bands
  • Fixed stderr buffering in CoreProcessRunner that caused stat parsing failures
  • Fixed algorithm dropdown highlight, status widget refresh, and settings persistence
  • Restructured README with screenshots, split out build guide and architecture docs
  • Added Java version compatibility guide
  • Updated javelin-cli submodule to v1.5.1-beta

0.3.3

  • Real-time PITest progress streaming in the progress dialog during Ochiai-MS analysis
  • Documented class name notation ($ for inner classes, # for methods) in Output Options
  • Renamed "javelin-core" to "javelin-cli" in the status bar readiness popup
  • Updated javelin-cli submodule to v1.5.0-beta

0.3.2

  • Fixed UnsupportedClassVersionError when project SDK is below Java 11 (e.g. Java 8 Defects4J projects)
  • Plugin now auto-detects project SDK version and falls back to JBR when SDK is too old for javelin-core

0.3.1

  • Timeout now caps the entire analysis (coverage, mutation, scoring) with remaining-time budgeting per phase
  • Fixed PITest minion timeouts on large projects by adding per-mutation timeout tuning
  • Added dedicated timeout exit code for clear error messages instead of generic "mutation failed"
  • Added "Build first" checkbox to skip IntelliJ compilation when building manually
  • Updated javelin-cli submodule to v1.4.1-beta

0.3.0

  • Cancel button now stops Ochiai/Ochiai-MS analysis and kills the process tree
  • Added configurable timeout (0-120 minutes) in Settings and tool window
  • Updated javelin-cli submodule to v1.4.0-beta

0.2.2

  • Fixed test discovery missing Test* prefix naming convention (e.g. JacksonDatabind)
  • Updated javelin-cli submodule to v1.3.1-beta

0.2.1

  • Added required-field asterisks to Granularity, Ranking, and Threads labels
  • Updated gutter icons and plugin icons
  • Updated javelin-cli submodule

0.1.2

  • Added statement-level and method-level granularity options
  • Added dense and average (MID) ranking strategies
  • Added persistent settings via Settings > Tools > Javelin
  • Added run statistics display (test counts, coverage, timing, mutation data)
  • Added plugin icons for light and dark themes
  • Expanded CSV export with percentile and band classification
  • Updated plugin description and documentation

0.1.1

  • Added JVM home override for Defects4J compatibility

0.1.0

  • Initial release with Ochiai and Ochiai-MS algorithms
  • 4-tier visual highlighting, results table, CSV export
  • Status-bar readiness widget and run configurations

action.description: Run fault localization on the current project
Clear highlights, gutter icons, stripe marks and result table
